title: Fermented Chewy Ukrainian Shrimp Bowl
description: A high‑protein, dairy‑free bowl featuring chewy buckwheat noodles, fermented miso‑shrimp, and crisp veggies for a post‑workout pick‑me‑up.
instructions: Rehydrate dried shrimp in warm water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Mix buckwheat flour, 200 ml water, and 1 tsp salt to form a smooth dough.
Roll dough to 3 mm thickness, cut into 2.5 cm wide ribbons, and boil in lightly salted water for 5 min until chewy; drain and rinse with cold water.
Whisk miso paste, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger to create the fermented sauce.
Heat a wok over medium‑high heat, add a splash of water, then stir‑fry the shrimp until opaque and 145 °F internal temperature is reached, about 2–3 min.
Add the sauce to the shrimp, stir, and cook for another 1–2 min until coated.
Toss the cooked noodles into the wok, heat for 1 min, ensuring they’re coated and warmed through.
Arrange the noodle‑shrimp mixture in bowls.
Top with julienned carrot and sliced green onions.
Serve immediately while warm; squeeze a splash of lime if desired.
